title: Cheap Houses for Rent in Old Brooklyn, Cleveland
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Old Brooklyn, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Cheap houses for rent in Old Brooklyn, Cleveland (44109) — rent ranges, transit near Pearl Road, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing today.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent Old Brooklyn cleveland
secondaryKeywords: low cost rentals near cleveland zoo
answerBox: Old Brooklyn, on Cleveland's southwest side in ZIP 44109, is among the more budget-friendly Cleveland neighborhoods for renting a house. Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent was $1,250 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report, and Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-friendly homes throughout the area.

sections.h2: Renting a cheap house in Old Brooklyn
body: Old Brooklyn is a southwest-side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44109**, running along the Big Creek valley near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo. Its rental stock is dominated by older single-family houses and two-family duplexes built in the early-to-mid 1900s on the side streets off Pearl Road and Broadview Road — smaller, older housing stock that tends to rent for less than newer apartment construction closer to downtown. Many of these homes share the neighborhood's original narrow-lot layout, with a front porch, a small side or rear yard, and a full basement.

Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-friendly rental homes in and around Old Brooklyn. [Check current availability](/p/book-showing) or see our [Old Brooklyn neighborhood page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) for more on the area.

sections.h2: What does a cheap house cost in Old Brooklyn?
body: Cleveland's citywide median asking rent was $1,250 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report, with the city running about 36% below the $1,950 national median that same month. Two-bedroom rentals citywide average around $1,100 a month, which is typically the lower end of what to expect for a house in Old Brooklyn; three-bedrooms citywide average closer to $1,350. Rent Finder Cleveland's own selection spans roughly $700 to $1,800 a month across the city, with a typical rent near $1,000.

sections.h2: Everyday life on a budget in Old Brooklyn
body: The Pearl Road and Broadview Road corridors carry most of the neighborhood's grocery stores, restaurants, and services, keeping day-to-day errands close to home without a long drive. The Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and Brookside Reservation border the neighborhood along Big Creek, offering free outdoor space within walking distance of many streets, and the West 25th Street corridor to the north links Old Brooklyn toward downtown and the RTA Red Line.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus service along Pearl Rd and Broadview Rd
**Highways:** Near the I-480/I-71 interchange for a car commute downtown
**Free outdoor space:** Cleveland Metroparks Zoo grounds and Brookside Reservation trails

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers in Old Brooklyn
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready, which can make Old Brooklyn a practical option for voucher holders looking for an affordable house. CMHA accepts preliminary voucher applications year-round through its online portal, with no fixed closing date, though selection runs through periodic mini-lotteries rather than a strict first-come, first-served list — so a name may come up within days or take months. Note that Ohio has no statewide requirement that landlords accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have a local source-of-income protection ordinance — see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full picture.

sections.h2: Older housing stock and lead-safe certification
body: Much of Old Brooklyn's rental stock was built in the early-to-mid 1900s, before lead-based paint was banned nationwide in 1978. Under Cleveland Codified Ordinances §365.04, every residential rental unit in the city built before January 1, 1978 must carry a current Lead-Safe Certification, renewed every two years following an independent evaluation. It's a reasonable, no-cost question to ask any Old Brooklyn landlord as part of comparing cheap rental options.

sections.h2: Stretching a tight rental budget in Old Brooklyn
body: Because Old Brooklyn's rents already sit near the lower end of Cleveland's citywide range, renters on a tight budget can often find a two-bedroom house here for close to $1,000 a month or less. For a side-by-side look at pricing across the whole city, our [Cleveland average rent guide](/cleveland-rentals/guides/average-rent-house-cleveland-2026/) compares 2026 figures by bedroom count, and [booking a showing](/p/book-showing) is the fastest way to see current listings before they're gone.

faq.q: What is the cheapest type of house to rent in Old Brooklyn?
faq.a: Two-bedroom houses tend to be the most affordable size in Old Brooklyn, generally near or below Cleveland's citywide two-bedroom average of about $1,100 a month (Zumper, July 2026). Exact pricing depends on condition, size, age of the home, and whether utilities are included in the lease.

faq.q: Is Old Brooklyn an affordable place to rent in Cleveland?
faq.a: Old Brooklyn's older housing stock generally rents for less than newer construction closer to downtown. Citywide, Cleveland's median rent was $1,250 a month as of Zumper's July 2026 report, and the city overall runs about 36% below the national median of $1,950 that same month.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Old Brooklyn in?
faq.a: Old Brooklyn is in ZIP code 44109 on Cleveland's southwest side, bordered by the Big Creek valley, the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo, and neighboring Brooklyn Centre, with the I-480/I-71 interchange a short drive to the north and the West 25th corridor connecting toward downtown.

faq.q: Are Old Brooklyn rentals Section 8 friendly?
faq.a: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including homes in and around Old Brooklyn. Ohio does not require all landlords to accept vouchers, and Cleveland itself has no local source-of-income protection ordinance, so availability and terms still vary by individual private property.

faq.q: How do I get to Old Brooklyn's shops and services?
faq.a: Most everyday shopping in Old Brooklyn is concentrated along Pearl Road and Broadview Road, both served by RTA bus routes connecting toward downtown. Drivers have quick access to the I-480/I-71 interchange a short distance north of the neighborhood, and the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo grounds sit along the eastern edge.
